attacking_style: Opportunistic from breakaways rather than direct GC attacks. Shows willingness to contest stage wins from reduced groups when freed from team duties, but lacks punch to sustain multi-day GC bids.
team_tactics: Deployed primarily as high-altitude domestique for INEOS leaders (Thomas), pacing on climbs before being released. Team utilizes his climbing strength in support role rather than as protected GC option.
terrain_preference: High-mountain stages where sustained tempo work is required. Benefits from reduced gruppo when allowed to follow wheels rather than set pace. Struggles with repeated explosive accelerations over multiple days.
vulnerability: Cannot recover between consecutive mountain stages—drops significantly from GC contention after expending energy on stage wins. Lacks durability for three-week GC campaigns despite individual climbing quality.
key_patterns: Consistently used as mountain domestique for INEOS leaders before personal result (3 observations)
Moves into top-10 GC via support work, not attacks—surplus strength pattern (2 observations)
Severe performance drop after peak efforts; 5-minute loss post stage win (2 observations)
Grand Tour participation disrupted by crashes/abandons, limiting sustained progress (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: Strong climbing engine evident in 2023 Giro support role, but Vuelta crash prevents assessment of improvement in multi-day resilience issues seen in 2022
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 17
2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 7
